steps.title: Set up the density formula
steps.content: We know that Density = Mass ÷ Volume, so we can rearrange to find Mass = Density × Volume. The density is 813 kg/m³, and we need to find the volume of a cube with edge length 0.60 meters.

steps.title: Calculate the cube's volume
steps.content:
For a cube with edge length $s$, the volume is $s^3$. With $s = 0.60$ meters:
$$V = (0.60)^3 = 0.60 \cdot 0.60 \cdot 0.60 = 0.216 \text{ m}^3$$

steps.title: Multiply density by volume to find mass
steps.content:
Now we apply the formula:
$$\text{Mass} = 813 \cdot 0.216 = 175.608 \text{ kg}$$
Rounding to the nearest whole number gives 176 kg. That matches choice A — A is correct.
